channel.title: crypto attacks — algoatson.github.io
channel.description: The signing oracle reused a nonce across two messages. That's the whole vulnerability — and it collapses the private key to a couple of modular divisions. The algebra, then the generalisation to merely *biased* nonces and a lattice.

item.title: the CBC padding oracle — decrypting without the key
item.description: A server that merely tells you whether a decrypted ciphertext had valid padding leaks the plaintext one byte at a time. No key, no brute force — just CBC's structure and a single bit of feedback turned into full decryption.

item.title: hash length extension — appending to a MAC you can't compute
item.description: H(secret ‖ message) looks like a MAC, but Merkle–Damgård hashes leak their internal state in the digest. Knowing that digest and the message length, you can resume the hash and append your own data — forging a valid tag for secret ‖ message ‖ padding ‖ anything, with the secret never in hand.

item.title: a tour of RSA attacks
item.description: RSA is sturdy; the ways people deploy it are not. A field guide to the classic breaks — tiny exponents, shared moduli, small private keys, careless primes, partial leaks — each one a parameter choice that turns "factor a 2048-bit number" into a few lines of arithmetic.

item.title: two signatures, one nonce: recovering an ECDSA key
item.description: The signing oracle reused a nonce across two messages. That's the whole vulnerability — and it collapses the private key to a couple of modular divisions. The algebra, then the generalisation to merely *biased* nonces and a lattice.
